title: AI Humor Generation: Cognitive, Social and Creative Skills for Effective Humor
research_questions: Does giving AI creative, social, and cognitive skills enable it to generate humorous captions that resonate with a target audience?
For Gen Z Instagram image caption humor, are HumorSkills captions rated funnier than captions from GPT-4o with prompt engineering?
Are HumorSkills captions rated as funny as the top-rated human-written Instagram comments?
Does the HumorSkills approach still outperform GPT-4o on non-target image sources such as camera-roll-style photos and museum art?

datasets_used.name: Instagram humor captioning accounts evaluation images and comments
Museum Art images
datasets_used.size: 30 museum art images: 15 images from the Museum of Modern Art (The MoMA) and 15 images of art from from the Metropolitan Art Museum (The Met)

methods_proposed.name: HumorSkills
methods_proposed.summary: HumorSkills is a multi-step system for Gen Z-style humorous image captioning. It uses GPT-4o for visual detail extraction, visual humor ideation, and narrative/conflict extrapolation, a fine-tuned GPT-3.5 model to generate captions with and without external narratives, and a GPT-4o-based Gen Z humor ranking agent to select the top five captions.

humor_theories.detail: The paper cites benign violation theory to motivate the idea that humor arises when expectations are violated in a surprising but not overly threatening, disturbing, or wrong way.

The paper discusses semantic script theory as describing how a setup leads listeners toward one interpretation while the punchline supports a different interpretation that is also consistent with the facts.

evaluation_methods.detail: Online surveys asked target-audience raters to rate captions from 1 to 5, where 1 was 'not funny', 3 was 'somewhat funny', and 5 was 'very funny'; image order and caption order were randomized and raters were not told which captions were AI-generated.
The target Instagram study used the top five most upvoted Instagram comments for each image as the human-written comparison condition.
Within HumorSkills, a GPT-4o-based Gen Z humor ranking agent ranked 30 generated captions by humor, relatability, and alignment with the image and narrative, then returned the top five.

headline_results.finding: On the target Instagram caption task, HumorSkills captions were rated 2.27 out of 5 and GPT-4o captions were rated 0.213 points lower, a statistically significant difference reported as p<0.0001.
headline_results.dataset: Instagram humor captioning accounts evaluation images and comments

headline_results.finding: On the same target task, top Instagram captions were only 0.08 points higher than HumorSkills on a 5-point scale, with p=0.053, so HumorSkills was not statistically less funny than the top Instagram captions.
headline_results.dataset: Instagram humor captioning accounts evaluation images and comments

headline_results.finding: The regression table for the target Instagram study reports 6015 observations, a HumorSkill intercept of 2.273, a GPT-4o coefficient of -0.213, and a Top5 IG coefficient of 0.078.
headline_results.value: No. Observations: 6015; Intercept 2.273; GPT-4o rating -0.213; Top5 IG rating 0.078
headline_results.dataset: Instagram humor captioning accounts evaluation images and comments

headline_results.finding: On camera-roll-style Flickr images, HumorSkills was rated 0.291 points higher than GPT-4o, reported in text as 2.19 vs. 1.9 out of 5 and p=0.022; Table 2 reports the HumorSkill coefficient as 0.291 with P>|z| of 0.000.

On museum art images, HumorSkills was rated 0.18 points higher than GPT-4o, reported in text as 2.18 vs. 2.00 out of 5 and p=0.023; Table 3 reports the HumorSkill coefficient as 0.175 with P>|z| of 0.000.
headline_results.value: 0.18 points; 2.18 vs. 2.00 out of 5; p=0.023; Table 3 coefficient 0.175; P>|z| 0.000

key_findings: HumorSkills was significantly funnier than GPT-4o with prompt engineering for Gen Z Instagram caption humor.
HumorSkills was rated almost on par with the top five upvoted Instagram comments and was not statistically less funny than them in the study sample.
HumorSkills also outperformed GPT-4o on non-target camera-roll-style Flickr images and museum art images.

The qualitative analysis attributes stronger captions to Gen Z slang and references, narrative extrapolation to relatable conflicts, and visual detail extraction that targets salient oddities in images.
The paper argues that detail observation, analogous social-situation generation, and audience modeling are human-like skills that can improve LLM humor generation.
limitations: The study targets only one form of humor for one audience: Gen Z-style Instagram image captions.
This humor style tends toward absurdities, which the paper says may be easier to generate than humor that must be both logical and surprising.
Caption humor is more well-defined than forms such as standup comedy or popular humor magazine writing because the image supplies the setup and the system only needs to generate a punchline.
The paper states that LLMs would likely struggle to make in-joke humor without inside information about friends, backgrounds, and shared experiences.
The GPT-4o baseline used a simple humor-writing prompt, and the paper notes that better prompts or multiple generations might produce similar results.
The paper did not conduct ablation studies, so it is unclear which HumorSkills components are most helpful individually.
Some generated captions are described as not logical enough to make sense but not illogical enough to be absurd, making them sound like mistakes.
safety_ethics_notes: The discussion notes that AI systems with human-like social skills such as humor could either augment human bonding or disingenuously create human bonding. It connects this concern to AI-generated persuasion, scams, dating-app message suggestions, and the broader need to distinguish genuine from disingenuous communication.
